title: Export Model
selectProject: Select a project with a trained model to export
noModel: No trained model found. Complete training first.

ollama.title: Export to Ollama
ollama.description: Convert your fine-tuned LoRA adapters into a standalone Ollama model for easy local use.
ollama.modelNameHint: Name for the model in Ollama (e.g. my-custom-model)
ollama.baseModelHint: Base model path or HuggingFace ID used during training
ollama.adapterHint: Select the training adapter to export, base model will be auto-filled
ollama.noAdapter: No trained adapter found. Complete training first.
ollama.successRunHint: Open your terminal and run the following command:
ollama.notInstalled: Ollama is not installed. Install from ollama.com first.

ollamaPathWarning: Configured Ollama models path '{{configuredPath}}' is missing the standard layout (manifests/registry.ollama.ai/library). Falling back to default path: {{fallbackPath}}
pathWarning: Configured export path '{{configuredPath}}' is unavailable (drive not mounted?). Files saved to local project directory: {{fallbackPath}}

gguf.title: Export as GGUF
gguf.description: Export as a .gguf file for use with llama.cpp, LM Studio, Jan, and other tools.
gguf.archNote: Supports Llama, Mistral, and Mixtral architectures only.

validation.needModelName: Please enter a model name first
validation.needQuantization: Please select a quantization method first

compat.title: Pre-export Check
compat.allGood: All checks passed — ready to export

keepFused.label: Also keep MLX model (for LM Studio)
keepFused.hint: Preserves the fused model directory alongside the Ollama export

mlx.title: Export as MLX Model
mlx.description: Export as an MLX safetensors model. Can be loaded directly by LM Studio (best performance on Mac) or served via local API.

server.title: Local Inference Server
server.description: Start an OpenAI-compatible API server from the exported MLX model. Any tool that supports the OpenAI API can connect.
server.noModel: Export an MLX model first to start the server.

guide.title: How to Use
guide.mlxSteps:
1. Open LM Studio → Load Model → Browse
2. Select the exported directory
3. Start chatting — native MLX performance on Mac
guide.serverSteps:
1. Click "Start Server" above
2. Connect any OpenAI-compatible tool:
   Base URL: {{endpoint}}
3. Works with: OpenWebUI, AnythingLLM, Chatbox, curl, etc.
guide.ggufSteps:
1. LM Studio: drag the .gguf file into the models folder
2. Jan: Import Model → select the .gguf file
3. llama.cpp: ./llama-cli -m <path_to_gguf>
